senses.gloss: to be familiar with (a person, a place, a piece of news or information, and so on); to know

examples.english: I know that she studied law.

senses.gloss: to be familiar with (a skill or profession); to be experienced in; to be proficient at or in; can

examples.english: I know how to sing and play music.

senses.gloss: to recognise (a person, a place, and so on); to identify; to recall

examples.english: I showed her his picture, but she could not recognize him.

senses.gloss: to become aware of; to find out

examples.english: That is when I knew he was lying.

senses.gloss: to tell about, to inform of, to make aware of; to acquaint with, to introduce to
a practice that is widely known somewhere or in some group; a convention; custom; a tradition
the crest or comb of a rooster, a cockscomb; any tuftlike protuberance; an outgrowth
the crest or comb of a rooster, a cockscomb; the forelock of a horse
